description_long: This object is a compact submachine gun featuring a predominantly black metal body with wooden elements on the stock and foregrip. The stock is fixed and made of wood, shaped ergonomically for shoulder support, and attached to the rear of the receiver. The foregrip is also wooden, providing a comfortable grip for the supporting hand. The barrel is short and cylindrical, with a perforated metal shroud for cooling, and lacks any visible muzzle attachments. The magazine is not visible, but a vertical magazine well is present beneath the receiver, suggesting a box magazine feed. Iron sights are present, with a simple rear aperture and a front post, both fixed to the top of the receiver and barrel.
description: A compact black metal submachine gun with wooden stock and foregrip, featuring a short perforated barrel and fixed iron sights.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, wooden stock, foregrip, and perforated barrel shroud.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wooden stock's shape and the alignment of the iron sights.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden components and the vertical magazine well.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the barrel opening and the perforated shroud.
attribute_barrel_type: short perforated cylindrical barrel with cooling shroud
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic contour
attribute_magazine_type: no magazine visible, vertical magazine well present
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: fixed rear aperture with fixed front post
